name: Touring the interior of La Vall d'Albaida — Circular from Ontinyent
name_original: Recorriendo el interior de La Vall d'Albaida — Circular desde Ontinyent
region_intro: Road cycling around Ontinyent features diverse natural landscapes, including the mountainous terrain of the Sierra de Mariola Natural Park and the Clariano River valley. The region is characterized by varied elevation changes, offering routes that traverse both challenging climbs and more undulating sections. Natural features such as the Pou Clar natural pools provide distinct landmarks along some routes. This area provides a network of roads suitable for various road cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around Ontinyent The most popular road cycling route is Touring the interior of La Vall d'Albaida — Circular from Ontinyent, a 36.5 miles (58.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 45 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the interior landscapes of the La Vall d'Albaida region. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Pou Clar Natural Pools – Pou Clar loop from Ontinyent, a difficult 42.2 miles (68.0 km) path. This route includes significant elevation gain and passes by the notable Pou Clar natural pools. Local road cyclists also love the Bellús Reservoir – Palacio de los Milà y Aragón loop from Ontinyent, a 51.1 miles (82.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain including the Bellús Reservoir, often completed in about 4 hours 2 minutes. Road cycling around Ontinyent is defined by mountainous landscapes, river valleys, and diverse natural features. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging climbs. The routes in Ontinyent are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 13 reviews. More than 2300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Ontinyent's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Ontinyent?
A: Ontinyent offers a diverse range of terrain for road cyclists. You'll find mountainous landscapes, particularly within the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Mariola Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, which provides challenging climbs and scenic roads. The region also features river valleys, such as the Clariano River, offering more undulating sections. While many routes involve significant elevation changes, there are also options for less strenuous rides.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginner road cyclists in Ontinyent?
A: Yes, Ontinyent has routes suitable for various skill levels. While many routes feature challenging climbs, there are also easier options. For example, the region's network includes 4 routes classified as easy, and 19 as moderate, offering a good starting point for less experienced riders or those looking for a more relaxed ride. The historical railway path, &lt;strong&gt;Vía Verde del Chicharra&lt;/strong&gt;, though sections are outside Ontinyent, indicates the potential for flatter, more accessible routes in the broader area.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along road cycling routes in Ontinyent?
A: Many routes in Ontinyent pass by stunning natural features. A notable highlight is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3485518" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pou Clar Natural Pools&lt;/a&gt;, a series of crystal-clear pools formed by the Clariano River, perfect for a refreshing stop. The routes also traverse the scenic &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Mariola Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, offering views of diverse flora and fauna, and peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1390751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montcabrer Summit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes available from Ontinyent?
A: Yes, Ontinyent serves as a great starting point for longer road cycling adventures. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6904602" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bellús Reservoir – Palacio de los Milà y Aragón loop from Ontinyent&lt;/a&gt; cover over 82 km, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34399497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Biar – Pou Clar Natural Area loop from Ontinyent&lt;/a&gt; extends to nearly 88.5 km with significant elevation gain. The &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Mariola Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt; also forms part of the long-distance GR-7 path, which can be explored by bike, offering extensive riding opportunities.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Ontinyent?
A: The region generally offers favorable conditions for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures and pleasant scenery. Summers can be hot, especially in July and August, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during these months. Winters are generally mild, making it possible to cycle year-round, though some higher elevation routes might be cooler.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes around Ontinyent?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Ontinyent are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e859594180/touring-the-interior-of-la-vall-d-albaida-circular-from-ontinyent" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Touring the interior of La Vall d'Albaida — Circular from Ontinyent&lt;/a&gt;, and the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5300839" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pou Clar Natural Pools – Pou Clar loop from Ontinyent&lt;/a&gt;. These loops offer varied distances and difficulties, catering to different preferences.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Ontinyent?
A: For routes that include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3485518" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pou Clar Natural Pools&lt;/a&gt;, parking is available at the municipal sports center, a couple of kilometers from the pools themselves. Generally, within Ontinyent, you can find public parking areas. For specific trailheads, it's advisable to check local signage or use navigation apps for designated parking spots near your chosen starting point.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Ontinyent?
A: The road cycling experience in Ontinyent is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 13 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse natural landscapes, which include both challenging mountainous sections and scenic river valleys. The variety of routes, from moderate to difficult, and the opportunity to visit natural landmarks like the Pou Clar pools, are frequently highlighted as key attractions.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or cultural sites accessible by road bike in the Ontinyent area?
A: Yes, road cycling routes in the Ontinyent area can lead you to various historical and cultural points of interest. While cycling, you might encounter structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2546615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cava Gran de Agres&lt;/a&gt;, an ancient snow cave, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4598481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santo Cristo&lt;/a&gt;. The broader region also features castles, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5518614" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Vallada&lt;/a&gt;, which can be part of longer rides, offering a blend of natural beauty and historical exploration.

Q: How many road cycling routes are there in and around Ontinyent?
A: The Ontinyent area is a popular destination for cyclists, offering an extensive network of routes. There are at least 164 cycling routes in and around the area, with 52 specifically featured in komoot's guides for road cycling. This provides a wide array of choices for different preferences and skill levels.

Q: Can I find challenging road bike climbs near Ontinyent?
A: Absolutely. The mountainous terrain of the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Mariola Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, which borders Ontinyent, is renowned for its challenging climbs. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34399497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Biar – Pou Clar Natural Area loop from Ontinyent&lt;/a&gt; feature over 1400 meters of elevation gain, making them ideal for experienced cyclists seeking significant ascents and rewarding descents.

Q: Are there amenities like cafes or rest stops along the road cycling routes?
A: While specific cafes are not listed for every route, many routes pass through or near towns and villages in the La Vall d'Albaida region, such as Salem, Muro de Alcoy, Agres, and Xàtiva. These settlements typically offer opportunities for rest stops, cafes, and other amenities where cyclists can refuel and take a break. It's always a good idea to plan your route and check for potential stops beforehand.
tips: Ontinyent is located in the south-west of the province of Valencia on the banks of the Clariano River and is landscaped by the mountainous relief of the Serra Grossa. Ontinyent has a very beautiful old town, in whose winding, narrow streets there is a lot to discover.
